name: Agonizing Relocation
description: You create a connection between a creature’s body and an unoccupied space within 10 feet of it. The target’s body is partially displaced toward that space, causing significant pain throughout their entire body. The target must attempt a Fortitude save. If the target fails or critically fails its save, it can choose not to be relocated, at the cost of taking double damage from the spell.

degrees.Success: The target takes 2d6 slashing damage.
degrees.Failure: The target takes 4d6 slashing damage and is moved to the linked space.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target takes 6d6 slashing damage and is moved to the linked space. Unlike most forced movement, a critical failure against agonizing relocation can move its target into a dangerous location.

heightened.4th: Increase the slashing damage to 3d6 on a success, 6d6 on a failure, or 9d6 on a critical failure. The spell’s range increases to 100 feet, and the unoccupied space chosen can be within 30 feet of the target.
heightened.6th: Increase the slashing damage to 4d6 on a success, 8d6 on a failure, or 12d6 on a critical failure. The spell’s range increases to 500 feet, and the unoccupied space chosen can be within 60 feet of the target.

name: Ancestral Memories
description: The memories of long-dead spellcasters grant you their knowledge, making your spells more formidable. You gain either a +1 status bonus to the next spell attack roll you attempt before the end of your turn or an enemy within 60 feet takes a –1 status penalty to the next saving throw they attempt against a spell you cast before the end of your turn.

heightened.5th: The bonus increases to +2 or the penalty increases to –2.
heightened.8th: The bonus increases to +3 or the penalty increases to –3.

name: Autumn's Howl
description: You call forth a chill wind that carries with it a barrage of fallen leaves and sharp twigs. All creatures in the area when you call the fallen leaves take 3d6 slashing damage. The winds persist for 1 minute. While the winds last, the area is difficult terrain for creatures both in the air and on the ground. Creatures that end their turn inside the wind take 1d6 persistent piercing damage.

heightened.+1: The damage from the initial blast increases by 2d6 and the persistent piercing damage for creatures ending their turn inside the wind increases by 1.

name: Bane
description: You fill the minds of your enemies with doubt. Enemies in the area must succeed at a Will save or take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls as long as they are in the area. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet and force enemies in the area that weren't yet affected to attempt another saving throw. Bane can counteract bless.

name: Banishment
targets: 1 creature that isn't on its home plane
description: You send the target back to its home plane. The target must attempt a Will save. You can spend an extra action while Casting this Spell and add a cost to the spell to give the creature a –2 circumstance penalty to its save. The cost must be a specially gathered object that is anathema to the creature. This spell fails if you aren't on your home plane when you cast it.

degrees.Critical Success: The target resists being banished and you are stunned 1.
degrees.Success: The target resists being banished.
degrees.Failure: The target is banished.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is banished and can't return by any means to the plane it's banished from for 1 week.

heightened.9th: You can target up to 10 creatures. The extra cost affects targets to which it is anathema.

name: Bewilder
description: Your magic overwhelms the target's senses, swapping faces, silhouettes, voices, and scents of nearby creatures with ones from their memories. The target believes everyone might be their friend and is wary of attacking the wrong creature. Any time they attempt a hostile action, they must attempt a DC 6 flat check, failing to perform the hostile action on a failure. If the creature fails to complete a hostile action against another creature due to this spell, it treats the creature it targeted with the hostile action as its ally until its next turn.

degrees.Success: The target is affected until the end of their next turn.
degrees.Failure: The target is affected for the duration of the spell, but it can attempt a Will save at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the spell ends.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is affected for the duration of the spell and the DC for the flat check is increased to 11.

heightened.8th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Blazing Bolt
description: You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

heightened.+1: The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2- and 3-action versions.

name: Bless
description: Blessings from beyond help your companions strike true. You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls while within the emanation. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet. Bless can counteract bane.

name: Blur
description: The target's form appears blurry. It becomes concealed. As the nature of this effect still leaves the target's location obvious, the target can't use this concealment to Hide or Sneak.

name: Boom-a-rang
description:
You conjure a boomerang of howling winds, then toss it at your foes. The boomerang sails from your hand to the end of the line.

Single Action The boomerang deals no damage on the initial throw. On your next turn, you can Sustain the spell to bring the boomerang back to your location. All creatures in that returning line take 2d8 sonic damage, with a basic Reflex save.

Two Actions As the 1-action version, except that the boomerang also harms creatures as soon as you throw it. Creatures in the line when you throw the boomerang also take 2d8 slashing damage, with a basic Reflex save.

Three Actions As the 2-action version, but the boomerang automatically returns to your location at the beginning of your next turn, without you having to Sustain it, and the sonic damage of the returning boomerang is increased to 2d10.

heightened.+1: Increase the sonic damage of the returning boomerang by 1d8 for the 1-action and 2-action versions and by 1d10 for the 3-action version. Increase the slashing damage from the 2-action and 3-action versions by 1d8.

name: Boulder Crush
area: 10-foot radius cylinder up to 40 feet tall
description: You conjure a large boulder in the air and send it crashing down to the ground. The boulder appears at the top of the cylinder before falling. Creatures underneath the boulder take 4d12 bludgeoning damage and must attempt a Reflex save.

degrees.Success: The creature takes half damage.
degrees.Failure: The creature takes full damage. If it was flying, it is repositioned to the bottom of the cylinder, landing on its feet without taking additional falling damage.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes full damage. If it was flying, it is repositioned to the bottom of the cylinder, landing on its feet without taking additional falling damage. If it is on the ground after the boulder’s fall, it is knocked prone. If it’s still in the air, it’s instead off-guard for 1 round or until it spends an action to right itself, whichever comes first.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d12 and the cylinder’s maximum height increases by 10 feet

riders.label: Boulder Crush: knocked prone
riders.condition: knocked prone if on the ground after the fall (off-guard 1 round if still flying)

name: Breathe Fire
description: A gout of flame sprays from your mouth. You deal 2d6 fire damage to creatures in the area with a basic Reflex save.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 2d6.

name: Buzzsaw
description:
You temporarily envelop your free hand in a metal casing covered with rows of whirling sawblades. Make a melee spell attack roll against your target’s AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 slashing damage plus 2 persistent bleed damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage and double bleed damage.
You can Sustain the spell for up to 1 minute. Each time you do, you can attempt a melee spell attack roll against a new target. While your hand remains covered in sawblades, it can’t be used for any other purpose.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d6 and the persistent bleed damage increases by 1.

name: Calm
description: You forcibly calm creatures in the area, soothing them into a nonviolent state; each creature must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: Calming urges impose a –1 status penalty to the creature's attack rolls.
degrees.Failure: Any emotion effects that would affect the creature are suppressed and the creature can't use hostile actions. If the target is subject to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by calm.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect.

name: Careless Swings
description: You use a carefully-timed illusion to cause your foe to leave a small opening in their defenses for your allies to take advantage of. Choose either the manipulate or move trait.

degrees.Success: The target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The first time the target Strikes before the end of its next turn, it gains the chosen trait.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except all of the target's Strikes gain the chosen trait until the end of its next turn.

name: Cascading Caltrops
description: You conjure forth a giant mass of jagged metal then break it into numerous caltrops that scatter to fill a 10-foot radius burst. When a creature enters a square in the area, they must attempt their choice of a Reflex save or Acrobatics check.

degrees.Success: The creature is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The caltrops dig into the creature, dealing 3d4 piercing damage and 3 persistent bleed damage. While the persistent bleed damage lasts, the creature takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speed. Spending an Interact action to pull the caltrops out reduces the DC of the flat check to stop the persistent bleed damage.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but double the piercing damage and persistent bleed damage and increase the status penalty to Speed to –10 feet. The field of caltrops lasts until a creature fails its Reflex save or Acrobatics check against the spell, you cast cascading caltrops again, or 1 minute passes, whichever comes first.

heightened.+1: Increase the piercing damage by 1d4 and the persistent bleed damage by 1.

name: Charitable Urge
description: You speak on the virtue of charity, compelling the target to give away its possessions. The target must attempt a Will save. If the target has no items on its person, the spell fails.

degrees.Success: The target is stunned 1 as it wrestles with the urge.
degrees.Failure: On its next turn, before it does anything else, the target must present the nearest creature with an item in its possession; the target chooses which item to give, and if the only item it has is one that it's currently using to defend itself, such as a weapon during a combat encounter, it can choose to be stunned for 1 round instead of giving up the item. This might require the target to Interact to retrieve an item or move to reach the nearest creature, and passing the item off requires an Interact action as normal.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the duration is 4 rounds, and the target must repeat the effects of a failure on each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, the target can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, ending the effects entirely on a critical success.

name: Charm
description: To the target, your words are honey and your visage seems bathed in a dreamy haze. It must attempt a Will save, with a +4 circumstance bonus if you or your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it. You can Dismiss the spell. If you use hostile actions against the target, the spell ends. When the spell ends, the target doesn't necessarily realize it was charmed unless its friendship with you or the actions you convinced it to take clash with its expectations.

degrees.Critical Success: The target is unaffected and aware you tried to charm it.
degrees.Success: The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of charm, unless it identifies the spell.
degrees.Failure: The target's attitude becomes friendly toward you. If it was already friendly, it becomes helpful. It can't use hostile actions against you.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target's attitude becomes helpful toward you, and it can't use hostile actions against you.

heightened.4th: The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations.
heightened.8th: The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Clairvoyance
description: You create an invisible floating eye at a location within range, even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect. While the eye can't move, you can see in all directions from that point as if using your normal visual senses.

name: Cleanse Affliction
description: Gentle restorative magic pushes back the effects of toxins and more complex maladies. Choose an affliction on the target, such as a curse, disease, or poison. If it has advanced past stage one, reduce the stage by one. This reduction can be applied only once to a given case of an affliction, with the case ending when it's completely cured. Although the reduction can't occur again, heightened versions of this spell attempt to counteract with each casting.

heightened.3rd: Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a disease or poison.
heightened.5th: Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a curse, disease, or poison.

name: Command
description: You shout a command that's hard to ignore. You can command the target to approach you, run away, release what it's holding, fall prone, or stand in place. The creature must succeed at a Will save or follow your command on its next turn. Some commands might require the target to Seek to approach you.

degrees.Success: The creature is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The creature must spend the first action on its next turn to obey your command.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target must use all its actions on its next turn to obey your command.

heightened.5th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Confusion
description: You befuddle your target with strange impulses, causing it to act randomly. The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell.

degrees.Success: The target babbles incoherently and is stunned 1.
degrees.Failure: The target is confused for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early.

heightened.8th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Counter Performance
trigger: You or an ally within 60 feet would be affected by an auditory or visual effect.
description: Your performance protects you and your allies. Roll a Performance check for a type you know—it must be an auditory performance if the trigger was auditory or a visual one for a visual trigger, and this action gains the traits for the type of performance (page 243). You and allies in the area can use the better result between your Performance check and the saving throw.

name: Create Food
description: You create enough food to feed six Medium creatures for a day. This food is bland and unappealing, but it is nourishing. After 1 day, if no one has eaten the food, it decays and becomes inedible. Most Small creatures eat one-quarter as much as a Medium creature (one-sixteenth as much for most Tiny creatures), and most Large creatures eat 10 times as much (100 times as much for Huge creatures and so on).

name: Creeping Cold
description: You cause frost creep and numb a creature's extremities. The target takes 1d6 persistent cold damage with a Fortitude save.

degrees.Success: The target takes half persistent damage and a –1 status penalty to their Reflex saves until the start of their next turn.
degrees.Failure: The target takes the full persistent damage and a –1 status penalty to their Reflex saves as long as they are taking this persistent damage.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but the status penalty is –2.

heightened.+1: The persistent damage increases by 1d6.

riders.label: Creeping Cold: Reflex penalty
riders.condition: –1 status penalty to Reflex saves (until the start of its next turn on a success; while taking the persistent damage on a failure)

riders.label: Creeping Cold: Reflex penalty
riders.condition: –2 status penalty to Reflex saves while taking the persistent damage

name: Crushing Stampede
description:
You briefly call forth a great stampede to batter your foes, with effects depending on the number of actions you spend. Affected creatures take bludgeoning damage and might be disoriented or knocked over based on the results of a Reflex save.

Single Action The stampede affects a single creature and deals 5d6 bludgeoning damage.

Two Actions The stampede affects all creatures in a 30-foot burst and deals 10d6 bludgeoning damage.

Three Actions You conjure a stampeding creature under each of your allies, allowing them to move to safety if they can manage to ride the perilous wave. As the 2-action version except allies in the area that succeed at their save against the spell take no damage and move to the edge of the spell’s radius in a direction of their choice. Allies that fail the saving throw experience the spell’s normal effects.

degrees.Success: The creature takes half damage.
degrees.Failure: The creature takes full damage and is off-guard for 1 round.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage and is knocked prone.

heightened.+1: Increase the damage of the 1-action version by 1d6 and the two-action and three-action versions by 2d6.

name: Darkvision
description: You grant yourself supernatural sight in areas of darkness. You gain darkvision.

heightened.3rd: The spell's range is touch and it targets 1 willing creature.
heightened.5th: The spell's range is touch and it targets 1 willing creature. The duration is until your next daily preparations.

name: Daze
description: You cloud the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage; the target must attempt a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also stunned 1.

name: Deafness
description: The target loses hearing; it must attempt a Fortitude save. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

degrees.Success: The target is deafened for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The target is deafened for 10 minutes.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is deafened permanently.

name: Detect Magic
description: You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

heightened.3rd: You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.
heightened.4th: As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

name: Detect Scrying
description: By reading trace auras, you detect the presence of scrying effects in the area. If detect scrying is higher rank than a scrying effect, you gain a glimpse of the scrying creature and learn its approximate distance and direction.

heightened.6th: The duration is until your next daily preparations.

name: Dispel Magic
targets: 1 spell effect or unattended magic item
description: You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a counteract check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, the item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an artifact or similar item, you automatically fail.

name: Dispelling Globe
description: You create an immobile globe around yourself that attempts to counteract any spell from outside the globe whose area or targets enter into the globe, as if the globe were a dispel magic spell 1 rank lower than its actual spell rank. If the counteract attempt succeeds, it prevents only the portion of the spell that would have entered the globe (so if the spell also has targets outside the globe, or part of its area is beyond the globe, those targets or that area is affected normally). You must form the sphere in an unbroken open space, so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost (though creatures can enter the globe after the spell is cast).

name: Dissonance
description: You induce deafening and nauseating vibrations throughout the bodies of all creatures in the area, dealing 5d10 sonic damage with a Fortitude save.

degrees.Success: The creature takes half damage and is deafened for 1d4 rounds.
degrees.Failure: The creature takes full damage, is sickened 1, and is deafened for 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage, is sickened 3, and is deafened for 1 hour.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d10.

name: Dizzying Colors
description: You unleash a swirling multitude of colors that overwhelms creatures based on their Will saves.

degrees.Success: The creature is dazzled for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The creature stunned 1, blinded for 1 round, and dazzled for 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is stunned for 1 round and blinded for 1 minute.

name: Drown
description: You summon a bubble of deoxygenated water around the target's face, gills, or wherever they use to breathe. Creatures that do not need to breathe are unaffected by this spell. Air bubble can counteract drown, and vice versa.

degrees.Success: The target takes a –2 status penalty to all checks it makes with the auditory or linguistics traits it attempts until the end of its turn.
degrees.Failure: The target must hold its breath or it begins suffocating. The target creature can Interact to attempt to remove the bubble, allowing it to attempt a new Fortitude save to end the effect.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the target also halves the number of rounds it can hold its breath (minimum 0).

name: Electric Arc
description: An arc of lightning leaps from one target to another. You deal 1d4+4 electricity damage to the target creatures. The targets must attempt a basic Reflex save.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d4.

name: Enthrall
description: Your words fascinate your targets. You speak or sing without interruption throughout the casting and duration. Targets who notice your speech or song might give their undivided attention; each target must attempt a Will save. The GM might grant a circumstance bonus (to a maximum of +4) if the target is of an opposing religion, ancestry, or political leaning, or is otherwise unlikely to agree with what you're saying. Each creature that comes within range has to attempt a save when you Sustain the spell. If you're speaking, enthrall gains the linguistic trait.

degrees.Critical Success: The target is unaffected and notices that you tried to use magic.
degrees.Success: The target needn't pay attention but doesn't notice you tried to use magic (it might notice others are enthralled).
degrees.Failure: The target is fascinated with you. It can attempt another Will save if it witnesses actions or speech with which it disagrees. If it succeeds, it's no longer fascinated and is temporarily immune for 1 hour. If the target is subject to a hostile act, or if another creature succeeds at a Diplomacy or Intimidation check against it, the fascination ends immediately.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but the target can't attempt a save to end the fascination if it disagrees with you.

name: Eye-Biting Shadow
description: You conjure a globe of frigid darkness and hurl it into the eyes of one of your foes.

degrees.Critical Success: The target takes 6d6 cold damage, and everything is concealed to them for 1 minute if they don’t have greater darkvision.
degrees.Success: The target takes 3d6 cold damage, and everything is concealed to them for 1 minute if they don’t have darkvision or greater darkvision. A target can end the concealment early by spending an action to wipe the spell’s residue out of their eyes; this action has the manipulate trait.

heightened.+1: The cold damage increases by 2d6 (or by 4d6 on a critical hit).

riders.label: Eye-Biting Shadow: concealment
riders.condition: everything is concealed to the target for 1 minute (negated by darkvision on a hit; only greater darkvision negates it on a crit)

name: False Vitality
description: You augment your flesh with the energies typically used to manipulate the undead. You gain 10 temporary Hit Points.

heightened.+1: The temporary Hit Points increase by 3.

name: Fashionista
description: The target's clothes are transformed into ostentatious attire that epitomizes high-end local fashion. No details of the target's appearance transform other than their clothes, so their weapons or armor remain unchanged in appearance. The target gains a +1 status bonus on Deception checks to Make a Distraction. You can Dismiss this spell. At the end of the spell's duration, the target's clothes revert to their original appearance.

heightened.+2: The status bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th level.

name: Fear
description: You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The target is frightened 1.
degrees.Failure: The target is frightened 2.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

heightened.3rd: You can target up to five creatures.

name: Figment
description: You create a simple illusory sound or vision. A sound adds the auditory trait to the spell and the sound can't include intelligible words or elaborate music. A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to Create a Diversion with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check. If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

name: Forbidding Ward
description: You ward an ally against the attacks and hostile spells from the target enemy. The target ally gains a +1 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws against the target enemy's attacks, spells, and other effects.

name: Force Barrage
description: You fire a shard of solidified magic toward a creature that you can see. It automatically hits and deals 1d4+1 force damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, increase the number of shards you shoot by one, to a maximum of three shards for 3 actions. You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

heightened.+2: You fire one additional missile with each action you spend.

name: Fountain of Blood
description: You cause the triggering creature's blood to geyser out in a horrific display of ultraviolence. Each other creature within 15 feet of the triggering creature must make a Will save. The target is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

degrees.Success: The creature is frightened 1.
degrees.Failure: The creature is frightened 2.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

name: Friendfetch
description: You shoot out ephemeral, telekinetic strands that drag each target directly toward you, stopping in the closest unoccupied space to you in this path. This is forced movement.

name: Glimmer of Charm
description: You're bathed in a smooth, almost glittering aura that improves the attitude of those near you. Any creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a Will saving throw with the following effects. No matter the result, it's then temporarily immune for 24 hours. The effect lasts until the spell ends, even after the creature leaves the aura.

degrees.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is aware of the aura.
degrees.Success: The creature's attitude toward you improves by one step. If that improves its attitude to at least friendly, it can't take hostile actions against you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies.
degrees.Failure: The creature's attitude toward you improves by two steps. It can't take hostile actions against you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature's attitude becomes helpful to you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies. While the creature is helpful, it can't take hostile actions against you.

name: Grease
targets: 4 contiguous 5-foot squares OR 1 object of 1 Bulk or less
description: You conjure grease.  All solid ground in the area is covered with grease. Each creature standing on the greasy surface must succeed at a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check against your spell DC or fall prone. Creatures using an action to move onto the greasy surface during the spell's duration must attempt either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to Balance. A creature that Steps or Crawls doesn't have to attempt a check or save. Alternatively, if you Cast the Spell on an unattended object, anyone trying to pick up the object must succeed at an Acrobatics check or Reflex save against your spell DC to do so. If you target an attended object, the creature that has the object must attempt an Acrobatics check or Reflex save. On a failure, the holder or wielder takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all checks that involve using the object; on a critical failure, the holder or wielder releases the item. The object lands in an adjacent square of the GM's choice. If you Cast this Spell on a worn object, the wearer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against attempts to grapple them.

name: Gruesome Marionettist
targets: 1 creature not immune to bleed damage
description: You seize control of the target's blood temporarily, using specific motions to move them to your will instead of their own. Choose two actions or activities the creature can take that have either the attack, manipulate, or move traits, one is prohibited and the other is directed. If the creature takes the prohibited action or activity, it takes 5d10 persistent bleed damage. If the creature takes the directed action or activity, it is immune to the bleed damage from this spell until the beginning of its next turn.

degrees.Success: The creature takes half the persistent bleed damage and the spell ends at the beginning of your next turn.
degrees.Failure: The creature takes the full persistent bleed damage and the spell lasts for the duration.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the creature takes double the persistent bleed damage.

heightened.+1: The persistent bleed damage increases by 1d10.

name: Guidance
duration: Until the start of your next turn
description: You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

name: Hallucination
description: The target consistently detects one thing as another, can't detect something that's there, or detects something that's not there, though it doesn't alter their beliefs. You choose which of these effects applies, and you determine the specifics of the hallucination. The target can attempt an initial Will save, with effects below. They also receive a Will save to disbelieve the hallucination every time they Seek or directly interact with the hallucination. The target can attempt to disbelieve with a large circumstance bonus in situations determined by the GM.

degrees.Success: The creature perceives what you chose until it disbelieves, but it knows what the hallucination is.
degrees.Failure: The creature perceives what you chose until it disbelieves.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature perceives what you chose until it disbelieves, and it trusts its false senses, taking a –4 circumstance penalty to saves to disbelieve.

heightened.6th: Choose to either target up to 10 creatures or change the spell's duration to until your next daily preparations.
heightened.8th: Choose to either target any number of creatures or change the spell's duration to unlimited.

name: Haste
description: Magic empowers the target to act faster. It gains the quickened condition and can use the extra action each round only for Strike and Stride actions.

heightened.7th: You can target up to 6 creatures.

name: Haunting Hymn
description: You echo a jarring hymn that only creatures in the area can hear. The hymn deals 1d8 sonic damage, with a basic Fortitude save. If a target critically fails the save, it's also deafened for 1 minute.

name: Hemophilia
description: You cause the target's blood to flow freely. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

degrees.Success: The target gains weakness 1 to bleed damage.
degrees.Failure: As success, except the first time the target takes bleed damage while the spell is active, it becomes drained 1 or increases its drained value by 1.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the weakness to bleed damage is doubled.

heightened.+1: The weakness to bleed damage increases by 1.

name: Heroism
description: You unlock the target's inner heroism, granting it a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks.

name: Honeyed Words
description: Falsehoods pass your lips as smoothly as silk. You gain a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to Lie and against Perception checks to discern if you are telling the truth, and you add your level even if untrained. If the implausibility of your lies prompts a circumstance penalty or a DC increase, reduce that penalty by half or increase it by half.

name: Hymn of Healing
description: Your glorious singing mends wounds and provides a temporary respite from harm. The target gains fast healing 2. When you Cast the Spell and the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, the target gains 2 temporary Hit Points, which last for 1 round.

heightened.+1: The fast healing and temporary Hit Points each increase by 2.

name: Hypnotize
description: You create a cloud of mesmerizing patterns and colors that hovers in the air. Creatures are dazzled while inside the cloud. In addition, a creature must attempt a Will saving throw if it is inside the cloud when you cast it, when it enters the cloud, when it ends its turn within the cloud, or if it uses a Seek or Interact action on the cloud. A creature currently fascinated by hypnotize doesn't attempt new saves.

degrees.Success: The target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The target is fascinated by the cloud.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is fascinated by the cloud. While it remains fascinated, it can't use reactions.

name: Illusory Creature
description: You create an illusory image of a Large or smaller creature. It generates the appropriate sounds, smells, and feels believable to the touch. If you and the image are ever farther than 500 feet apart, the spell ends. The image can't speak, but you can use your actions to speak through the creature, with the spell disguising your voice as appropriate. You might need to attempt a Deception or Performance check to mimic the creature, as determined by the GM. This is especially likely if you're trying to imitate a specific person and engage with someone that person knows. In combat, the illusion can use 2 actions per turn, which it uses when you Sustain the spell. It uses your spell attack modifier for attack rolls and your spell DC for its AC. Its saving throw modifiers are equal to your spell DC – 10. It is substantial enough that it can flank other creatures. If the image is hit by an attack or fails a save, the spell ends. The illusion can cause damage by making the target believe the illusion's attacks are real, but it cannot otherwise directly affect the physical world. If the illusory creature hits with a Strike, the target takes 3d4 mental damage. The illusion's Strikes are nonlethal. If the damage doesn't correspond to the image of the monster—for example, if an illusory Large dragon deals only 5 damage—the GM might allow the target to attempt an immediate Perception check to disbelieve the spell. Any relevant resistances and weaknesses apply if the target thinks they do, as judged by the GM. For example, if the illusion wields a warhammer and attacks a creature resistant to bludgeoning damage, the creature would take less mental damage. However, illusory damage does not deactivate regeneration or trigger other effects that require a certain damage type. The GM should track illusory damage dealt by the illusion. Any creature that touches the image or uses the Seek action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion. When a creature disbelieves the illusion, it recovers from half the damage it had taken from it (if any) and doesn't take any further damage from it.

heightened.+1: The damage of the image's Strikes increases by 1d4, and the maximum size of creature you can create increases by one (to a maximum of Gargantuan).

name: Illusory Disguise
description: You create an illusion that causes the target to appear as another creature of the same body shape, and with roughly similar height (within 6 inches) and weight (within 50 pounds). The disguise is typically good enough to hide their identity, but not to impersonate a specific individual. The spell changes their appearance and voice, but not mannerisms. You can change the appearance of its clothing and worn items, such as making its armor look like a dress. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance. Casting illusory disguise counts as setting up a disguise for the Impersonate use of Deception; it ignores any circumstance penalties the target might take for disguising itself as a dissimilar creature, gives a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to prevent others from seeing through the disguise, and lets the target add its level to such Deception checks even if untrained. You can Dismiss this spell.

heightened.3rd: The target can appear as any creature of the same size, even a specific individual. You must have seen an individual to replicate its appearance, and must have heard its voice to replicate its voice.
heightened.4th: You can target up to 10 willing creatures. If you target multiple creatures, you can choose a different disguise for each target, but none can impersonate a specific individual. You can Dismiss each disguise individually or all collectively.
heightened.7th: As 4th, but you can choose disguises that impersonate specific individuals. You must have seen an individual to replicate its appearance, and must have heard its voice to replicate its voice.

name: Illusory Object
description: You create an illusory visual image of a stationary object. The entire image must fit within the spell's area. The object appears to animate naturally, but it doesn't make sounds or generate smells. For example, water would appear to pour down an illusory waterfall, but it would be silent. Any creature that touches the image or uses the Seek action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

heightened.2nd: our image makes appropriate sounds, generates normal smells, and feels right to the touch. The spell gains the auditory and olfactory traits. The duration increases to 1 hour.
heightened.5th: As the 2nd-rank version, but the duration is unlimited.

name: Illusory Scene
description: You craft an imaginary scene that includes up to 10 discrete creatures or objects of various sizes, all of which must be within the spell's area. These elements generate appropriate sounds and smells, and they feel right to the touch. Elements of an illusory scene are incapable of speech. Unlike with illusory creature, creatures in your scene lack combat abilities and statistics. When you create the scene, you can choose to have it be static or follow a program of up to 1 minute that repeats when finished. Any creature that touches any part of the image or Seeks to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion. If they interact with a portion of the illusion, they disbelieve only that portion. They disbelieve the entire scene only on a critical success.

heightened.6th: Creatures or objects in your scene can speak. You must speak the specific lines for each actor when creating your program. The spell disguises your voice for each actor.
heightened.8th: As the 6th-rank version, and the duration is unlimited.

name: Infectious Enthusiasm
description: With enchanted gusto, you encourage yourself to get things done and share your motivation with your allies. You gain a +1 status bonus to your choice of attack rolls, Will saves, or Charisma-based skill checks. During the spell's duration, an ally who observed you Casting this Spell and was within 30 feet when you did so can take a single action, which has the concentrate trait, to gain the same bonus you chose until the start of its next turn. Using this spell to influence someone in a social situation is typically considered a faux pas, negating the benefit on Charisma-based skills if the subject of the check observed you casting the spell.

name: Infectious Melody
description: You call forth the chords of a tune so catchy that foes hearing it can't help but sing along, making it harder for them to communicate. Each target must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: For 1 round, the creature can communicate only by singing along to the melody. It can speak only on its turn, and it must take the Perform action when it does. It still communicates the information regardless of the Performance check result. This effect might interfere with verbal components of spells or other linguistic abilities that rely on precise speech; to use such an ability successfully, the creature must succeed at a flat check. The creature also takes a –2 status penalty on linguistic Intimidation checks, and its constant humming makes it nearly impossible for the creature to remain undetected.
degrees.Failure: As success, but the duration is 3 rounds.
degrees.Critical Failure: As success, but the duration is 1 hour.

name: Inner Upheaval
description: You focus your qi into devastating magical attacks. Make an unarmed Strike or Flurry of Blows (this doesn't change the limit on using only one flourish per turn). You gain a +1 status bonus to your attack rolls with the Strikes, and the Strikes deal 1d6 extra damage. This damage can be any of the following types of your choice, chosen each time you Strike: force, spirit, vitality, or void.

name: Inspire Courage
description: You inspire your allies with words or tunes of encouragement. You and all allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects.

name: Instant Parade
description: An illusory parade with dozens of participants and performers appears around you, following you as you move. The parade is lively and noisy, providing enough cover and distraction to hide among the crowd. You and other creatures can Hide and Sneak inside the crowd, though creatures that disbelieve the illusion still see creatures within as normal. You can choose to send the parade off with a 2-action activity, which has the concentrate trait. When you do so, the parade no longer follows you and instead continues traveling in the direction of your choice. The parade travels 100 feet over 1 round and then disappears as the spell is Dismissed. You can otherwise Dismiss the spell normally if you prefer.

name: Internal Distortion
description: You create a spatial ripple inside of a creature’s body, injuring it from within. The target takes 1d6 slashing damage, with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also sickened 1.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d6.

name: Invisibility
description: Illusions bend light around the target, rendering it invisible. This makes it undetected to all creatures, though the creatures can attempt to find the target, making it hidden to them instead. If the target uses a hostile action, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

heightened.4th: The spell lasts 1 minute, but it doesn't end if the target uses a hostile action.

name: Item Facade
targets: 1 object of up to 10 feet by 10 feet by 10 feet
description: You make the target object look and feel as though it were in much better or worse physical condition. When you Cast the Spell, decide whether you want to make the object look decrepit or perfect. An item made to look decrepit appears broken and shoddy. An intact item made to look better appears as though it's brand new and highly polished or well maintained. A broken item appears to be intact and functional. Destroyed items can't be affected by this spell. A creature that Interacts with the item can attempt to disbelieve the illusion.

name: Kinetic Tow
description: You bind a creature with chains that swiftly contract. The target creature is drawn by an invisible chain towards another creature or object of your choice within 30 feet of the target, depending on its Fortitude save. This is forced movement.

degrees.Critical Success: The target is unaffected and the spell ends.
degrees.Success: The target is moved 10 feet towards the creature or object and then the spell ends.
degrees.Failure: The target is moved 15 feet towards the creature or object and cannot move more than 30 feet from the creature. The creature can attempt a new Fortitude save against the effect by spending an Interact action to break the chain. The target can choose to use its modifier in Athletics for its Fortitude save.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the target is moved 30 feet.

name: Knock
description: You make the target easier to open. Knock grants a +4 status bonus to any creature that tries to open the target door, lock, or container with an Athletics or a Thievery check. You can attempt a Thievery check to open the target as part of casting knock, and you add your level even if you're untrained. Knock counteracts Lock.

name: Knock Heads
targets: 2 creatures that are up to 40 feet apart
description: Choose two creatures in the area to telekinetically grab and smash into each other. Both targets are repositioned to the midpoint between them and battered against each other, taking 16d6 bludgeoning damage. If either creature critically succeeds at their saving throw, the other creature takes no damage from the spell.

degrees.Success: The creature is repositioned and takes half damage.
degrees.Failure: The creature is repositioned and takes full damage.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is repositioned and takes double damage.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 2d6 and the maximum distance between the two targets increases by 5 feet.

name: Laughing Fit
description: The target is overtaken with uncontrollable laughter. It must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The target is plagued with uncontrollable laughter. It can't use reactions.
degrees.Failure: The target is slowed 1 and can't use reactions.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target falls prone and can't use actions or reactions for 1 round. It then takes the effects of a failure.

name: Launching Vortex
description: You call forth a vortex around your legs, shredding everyone nearby before redirecting the vortex’s energy to propel you into the air. When you cast the spell, all adjacent creatures take 4d6 slashing damage, with a basic Reflex save. You then jump 30 feet in any direction without touching the ground. You must land on a space of solid ground within 30 feet of you, or else you fall after using your next action. On your future turns until the spell’s duration expires, you can recreate the vortex once per round as a single action with the move and manipulate traits, dealing the slashing damage and jumping up to 30 feet each time you do.

heightened.+1: Increase the slashing damage by 1d6 and the maximum distance of the jump by 5 feet.

name: Light
duration: Until the next time you make your daily preparations
description: You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement. You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

heightened.4th: The orb sheds light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light for the next 60 feet).

name: Lingering Composition
description: You add a flourish to your composition to extend its benefits. If your next action is to cast a cantrip composition with a duration of 1 round, attempt a Performance check. The DC is standard-difficulty of a level equal to the highestt level target of your composition.

degrees.Success: The composition lasts 3 rounds.
degrees.Failure: The composition lasts 1 round, but you don't expend a focus point for trying.

name: Localized Eclipse
description: You draw a curtain of darkness over the celestial firmament. In the area, bright light becomes dim light and dim light becomes darkness. The first time you Sustain the spell each turn, you can move the area to another location within 30 feet.

heightened.+1: You increase the area of the burst by 5 feet.

name: Lucky Decision
description:
You know that destiny and fate are not something that is predetermined. As part of casting this spell roll a d20 and make note of the result. For the duration of the spell you gain the Your Good Luck and Their Bad Luck reactions. If you use either reaction the spell ends immediately.

Your Good Luck (reaction, manipulate) Trigger You are about to attempt a non-secret attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. Effect You insert the fate you have made for yourself. Instead of rolling a d20, you use the result you rolled when casting this spell for your roll. This is a fortune effect.

Their Bad Luck (reaction, manipulate) Trigger An enemy creature is about to attempt an attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. Effect You steal the creature’s fate and insert your own. The target must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The target rolls their check with a –2 status penalty.
degrees.Failure: The target uses your roll from this spell for the triggering roll and takes a –2 status penalty to the check.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the status penalty is –4.

name: Mind Games
description: You lock minds with the target, trying to mentally subdue them. The target must attempt a Will save. Each time you Sustain this Spell, the target attempts another Will save.

degrees.Critical Success: You are stunned 1 and the spell ends.
degrees.Success: The target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The target is stunned 1.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is stunned 2.

name: Mind Probe
description: You cast your thoughts through a creature's mind, sifting for information. The target must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: Each round of the spell's duration, you can Sustain the spell to ask a different question and attempt to uncover the answer. For each question, the target can attempt a Deception check against your spell DC; if the target succeeds, you don't learn the answer, and on a critical success, the target gives you a false answer that you believe is truthful. Once you've asked the target a given question, asking it again, even with a separate casting of mind probe, produces the same result.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, and the target takes a –4 circumstance penalty to Deception checks against your questions.

name: Mind Reading
duration: 1 round or Sustained up to 1 minute
description: With a cursory mental touch, you attempt to read the target's mind. It must attempt a Will save. The target then becomes temporarily immune to your mind reading for 1 hour.

degrees.Critical Success: The target perceives vague surface thoughts from you when you Cast the Spell.
degrees.Success: You find out whether the target's Intelligence modifier is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.
degrees.Failure: You perceive vague surface thoughts from the target when you Cast the Spell, and you find out whether its Intelligence is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, and for the duration of the spell, you can Sustain the spell to detect the target's surface thoughts again. The target doesn't receive any additional saves.

name: Mirror Image
description: Three illusory images of you swirl about your space, potentially causing those who attack you to hit one of the images instead of you. Any attack that would hit you has a random chance of hitting one of your images instead of you. If all three images remain, there is a 1 in 4 chance of hitting you (1 on 1d4). With two images remaining, there is a 1 in 3 chance of hitting you (1–2 on 1d6). With only one image, the chances are 1 in 2 (1–3 on 1d6). Once an image is hit, it is destroyed. If an attack roll fails to hit your AC but doesn't critically fail, it destroys an image but has no additional effect (even if the attack would normally have an effect on a failure). If an attack roll is a critical success and would hit one of the images, one of the images is destroyed and the attack roll becomes a success against you. Once all the images are destroyed, the spell ends.

name: Murmured Prayer (+2 Guidance)
description: You utter a prayer to a particularly relevant deity as part of casting Guidance: the target gains a +2 status bonus instead of +1 to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check before the start of your next turn. If the deity's goals do not align with your own, they may refuse to aid you. The target is immune to further Guidance for 1 hour either way.

name: Mystic Armor
duration: Until the next time you make your daily preparations
description: You ward yourself with shimmering magical energy, gaining a +1 item bonus to AC and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5. While wearing mystic armor, you use your unarmored proficiency to calculate your AC.

heightened.4th: You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.6th: The item bonus to AC increases to +2, and you gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.8th: The item bonus to AC increases to +2, and you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.10th: The item bonus to AC increases to +3, and you gain a +3 item bonus to saving throws.

name: Noise Blast
description: A cacophonous noise blasts out, dealing 2d10 sonic damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save.

degrees.Success: The target takes half damage.
degrees.Failure: The target takes full damage and is Deafened for 1 round
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage and is deafened for 1 minute, and stunned 1.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d10.

name: Overselling Flourish
description: You make a grand spectacle out of getting hit. Enhanced by magic, this spectacle features sprays of blood, anguished screams, or other theatrics that appear to result from your foe's attack. The triggering creature must attempt a Will saving throw.

degrees.Success: The creature is thrown off by your display. The creature becomes dazzled until the start of your turn.
degrees.Failure: The creature fully believes your performance, leaving itself open. The creature becomes dazzled and flat-footed until the start of your turn.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is enraptured by the display. The creature uses its remaining actions to watch you in awe. It then remains dazzled and flat-footed until the start of your turn.

name: Painful Vibrations
description: You send powerful sound waves through an opponent's body, vibrating its bones and internal organs painfully. The creature takes 8d6 sonic damage and must attempt a Fortitude save.

degrees.Success: The target takes half damage.
degrees.Failure: The target takes full damage, is sickened 1, and is deafened for 1 round.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target takes double damage, is sickened 2, and is deafened for 1 minute.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 2d6.

name: Paralyze
description: You block the target's motor impulses before they can leave its mind, threatening to freeze the target in place. The target must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The target is stunned 1.
degrees.Failure: The target is paralyzed for 1 round.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is paralyzed for 4 rounds. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, or end it entirely on a critical success.

heightened.7th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Phantasmal Treasure
description: A phantasmal image of the most precious thing imaginable to the target appears in a location of your choice within the spell's range. Only the spell's target can see the treasure, though you can see the vague shape of the treasure—be it a pile of items, a deific avatar, or a cherished loved one or hero. The target's response to the treasure is based on the outcome of the target's Will save.

degrees.Success: The target becomes fascinated with the treasure, and the duration is until the end of its turn. The target can also try to disbelieve the illusion if it touches the treasure, Seeks to examine it, or speaks to it if the illusion appears to be a person or the like. If the target disbelieves the illusion, the spell ends.
degrees.Failure: As success, but the duration is 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: As success, but the duration is 1 minute. The target finds the treasure so appealing that until the spell ends, it must spend each action focused on it. This can include moving toward the treasure if the target isn't next to it, and Interacting with the treasure if the target is next to it.

name: Phantom Prison
description: You completely surround a Large or smaller creature in immobile illusory walls, trapping it inside a false prison it can't escape. No other creatures see or feel these walls, and the target can't see anything outside of the illusory walls. The target can attempt a Will save when the spell is cast, each time it attempts to interact with or escape the walls, and each time something from outside the walls affects the target. On a success, it disbelieves the illusion and the spell ends.

heightened.8th: You can target up to 5 creatures.

name: Pocket Library
description: When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you are at least trained that has the Recall Knowledge action. During the duration of this spell, you can call forth a tome from an extradimensional library called the Akashic Record when attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must have a hand free to do so. The tome appears in your hand, open to an appropriate page. This grants you a +1 status bonus to the Recall Knowledge check. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the roll is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference a book from your pocket library, the spell ends.

heightened.3rd: The status bonus increases to +2 and you can reference your pocket library twice before the spell ends.
heightened.6th: The status bonus increases to +3 and you can reference your pocket library three times before the spell ends.
heightened.9th: The status bonus increases to +4 and you can reference your pocket library four times before the spell ends.

name: Prestidigitation
description: The simplest magic does your bidding. You can perform simple magical effects for as long as you Sustain the Spell. Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can choose any one of four options: cook, lift, clean, or color. Prestidigitation can't deal damage or cause adverse conditions. Any actual change to an object (beyond what is noted above) persists only as long as you Sustain the spell.

name: Propagating Arc
description: Cackling energy flies from your fingertips leaping through targets until the area around the target is filled with electricity. You create an arc of electricity that deals 2d12 electricity damage. If the target fails its save, each creature within 10 feet of the target also takes 2d6 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save, or half this amount if the creature was further than 10 feet but within 20 feet of the target. There must be at least 1 creature within 10 feet of the target for you to deal damage to creatures other than the target.

heightened.+1: The damage to the target increases by 1d12 and the damage to targets within 10 feet of the target increases by 1d6.

name: Pummeling Shockwave
description: You wreathe your body in magical energy, preparing to introduce a distant opponent to the taste of your martial arts. When your fist surges forth or your claws slash, the shockwaves they create tear into your foe’s body as surely as if you were standing right next to them. Make a melee Strike with an unarmed attack using your typical attack bonus for the unarmed attack or your spell attack bonus, whichever is better, against a foe within 30 feet. If the Strike hits, it deals its normal damage.

heightened.5th: The range increases to 120 feet, and your attack ignores concealment.
heightened.7th: The range increases to 400 feet, and your attack ignores both cover and concealment.

name: Quicken Time
description: You speed up the time stream in an area, stretching and pulling it until time flows swift and wild. Any creature that begins its turn in the area is quickened until the end of its turn and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. Unfortunately, speeding up time is much more difficult than slowing it down, and the effect is uneven and jittery, making accuracy between times painfully difficult; creatures inside the area are concealed to those outside it, and vice versa.

name: Raise the Reaper's Scythe
description: You conjure a spectral scythe that gradually phases into reality above a target’s head and follows them wherever they go. At the end of the creature’s next three turns, an ominous bell tolls, and the creature must succeed at a Will save or be frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure); this effect has the emotion, fear, and mental traits. After the third toll, the scythe swings, striking the creature in the neck and dealing 10d10 slashing damage with a basic Fortitude save. If the spell reduces a target to 0 Hit Points, they die; this is a death effect.

heightened.+1: Increase the damage by 2d10.

name: Ray of Frost
description: You blast an icy ray. Make a spell attack roll. The ray deals 2d4 cold damage.

degrees.Critical Success: The target takes double damage and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d4.

name: Reforge Weapon
description: You temporarily adjust the shape and weight of a metal weapon to adapt it to suit its wielder’s needs. Apply one of the following weapon traits to the weapon: disarm, grapple, nonlethal, shove, trip, versatile B, versatile P, versatile S.

heightened.4th: You can apply two of the listed weapon traits to the weapon or apply one of backstabber or sweep.
heightened.6th: You can apply three of the listed weapon traits to the weapon or one of backstabber, backswing, forceful, deadly d8, or sweep.

name: Reverberating Pain
targets: 1 creature that has taken damage since the beginning of your last turn
description: You pull an echo of your target’s recent injuries through time, inflicting them anew. Choose a type of damage that the target has taken since the beginning of your last turn. The target takes 2d6 damage of that type.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d6.

name: Ring of Truth
description: You designate an area in which lies are revealed. Creatures in the area also take a –2 status penalty to Deception checks. Each time a creature in the area speaks a true statement, the soft ring of a bell sounds in the area. Creatures are aware of the magic; therefore, they can avoid answering questions to which they would normally respond with a lie, or they can be evasive as long as they remain within the boundaries of the truth. If a creature is in the area when the spell is cast or later enters the area, that creature attempts a Will save. It uses the results of this initial save if it leaves and reenters the area.

degrees.Critical Success: The target is so convincing that the bell rings even if they lie.
degrees.Success: If the target lies and succeeds at their Deception check against all targets, the bell still rings.
degrees.Failure: The bell accurately sees through their deception and will never ring if they lie.

name: Runic Body
description: Glowing runes appear on the target's body. All its unarmed attacks become +1 striking unarmed attacks, gaining a +1 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the number of damage dice to two.

heightened.6th: The unarmed attacks are +2 greater striking.
heightened.9th: The unarmed attacks are +3 major striking.

name: Rushing Gust
description: You stir up the air around you to propel yourself toward a foe, then slice into them with blades of air. Move up to half your speed toward a target, then make a melee spell attack roll against the target’s AC. If you hit, the target takes 2d4 slashing damage (double damage on a critical hit).

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d4.

name: Sandstorm
description: You call forth a localized sandstorm that cuts at the eyes and chokes the breath of creatures within it. All creatures within the sandstorm become concealed, and all creatures outside the sandstorm become concealed to creatures within it. A creature that starts its turn in the sandstorm or enters the sandstorm takes 6d6 slashing damage with a basic Reflex save. A creature that ends its turn in the sandstorm must hold its breath to avoid suffocation. You can Dismiss the sandstorm.

heightened.+1: The slashing damage from the sandstorm increases by 1d6.

name: Scouting Eye
description: You create an invisible, floating eye that is 1 inch in diameter, at a location you can see within 500 feet. The eye can see in all directions with your normal visual senses, and it continuously transmits what it sees to you. The first time you Sustain the spell each round, you can move the eye up to 30 feet if you're concentrating on seeing only things in front of the eye, or up to 10 feet if you want to see in all directions around the eye. There's no limit to how far away from you the eye can move, but the spell ends immediately if you and the eye are ever on different planes of existence. You can attempt Seek actions through the eye if you want to attempt Perception checks with it. The eye is destroyed if it takes any damage, ending the spell.

name: See the Unseen
description: Your gaze pierces through illusions and finds invisible creatures and spirits. You can see invisible creatures as though they weren't invisible, although their features are blurred, making them concealed and difficult to identify. You can also see incorporeal creatures, like ghosts, phased through an object from within 10 feet of an object's surface as blurry shapes seen through those objects. Subtler clues also grant you a +2 status bonus to checks you make to disbelieve illusions.

name: Serrate
description: With a pinch, the metal of a weapon twists, adding numerous jagged blades along its surface. Strikes with the target weapon deal an additional 1d4 slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

name: Shade of Night
description: Plunging creatures into darkness to conceal yourself and your allies or to confuse your enemies. The target creature attempts a Fortitude save, reducing their ability to see in darkness or low-light. Depending on their save, the target reduces their darkvision to low-light vision, and low-light vision to no special vision. If the creature has light sensitivity, it is suppressed for the duration. If the target is a willing ally, the ally can choose which result it gets without rolling.

degrees.Success: The target's vision is reduced for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The target's vision is reduced for 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the target is also blinded for 1 round.

heightened.2nd: The spell reduces a creature's darkvision and low-light vision to no special vision.
heightened.4th: The spell reduces greater darkvision to low-light vision in addition to its other effects.

name: Shadow's Betrayal
targets: 1 creature wielding a weapon or unarmed attack
description: You inject a small amount of magic into a foe's shadow, causing it to rise off the ground and attack its caster. Choose a weapon the target is wielding or an unarmed attack it could use for a Strike and make a spell attack roll against the target's AC. If the target is in dim light or darkness, they are off-guard to this spell attack roll. If you hit, you deal 2d8 damage to the creature of the same damage type as the chosen weapon or unarmed attack. If the chosen weapon or unarmed attack you chose deals multiple types of damage, you choose which damage type when you make your spell attack roll.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d8.

name: Shared Invisibility
targets: you and up to 5 willing creatures
description: You and all targets are invisible except to each other as long as the targets remain within the emanation. If a creature made invisible by this spell leaves the spell's area, it becomes visible and remains so even if it returns to the spell's area. If any creature made invisible by this spell uses a hostile action, the spell ends after the hostile action is completed.

heightened.5th: The targets increase to you and up to 10 willing creatures. The duration increases to 1 hour.

name: Shatter
description: A high-frequency sonic attack shatters a nearby object. You deal 2d10 sonic damage to the object, ignoring the object's Hardness if it is 4 or lower.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d10, and the Hardness the spell ignores increases by 2.

name: Shield
duration: Until the start of your next turn
description: You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use. While the spell is in effect, you can use the Shield Block reaction with your magical shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

name: Shields of the Spirit
description:
You Raise your Shield, causing ephemeral spirit shields to float within your champion's aura. The shields last until the start of your next turn or until you're no longer raising your shield, whichever comes first. While one of your allies is in your champion's aura, the shields grant them a +1 status bonus to AC, and each time an enemy makes an attack against the ally, the enemy takes 1d4 spirit damage (even if it misses).
The benefit applies only while an ally is in your aura, ending for any ally that leaves and applying to any that enters later. As normal, you don't count as your own ally and therefore don't get the benefits of the spirit shields yourself.

name: Shift Blame
trigger: You or another creature attacks a creature or fails at a Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation check.
targets: the target of the triggering attack or skill check
description: You alter the target's memories of the triggering event as they form. You choose another creature (which can be you) with the capacity to make the triggering attack or skill check, and you alter the target's memories to recall the creature you chose as responsible for the triggering attack or skill check. The target must attempt a Will save and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.

degrees.Critical Success: The target knows you attempted to alter its memories.
degrees.Success: The target doesn't realize you attempted to alter its memories, though it knows you cast a spell.
degrees.Failure: You successfully alter the target's memory. It isn't forced to react to the new memories in a particular way, and it's likely to question them if they contradict other information it knows or are implausible for the situation.

name: Shocking Grasp
description: You shroud your hands in a crackling field of electricity. Make a melee spell attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 electricity damage. If the target is wearing metal armor or is made of metal, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll with shocking grasp, and the target also takes 1d4 persistent electricity damage on a hit.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d12, and the persistent electricity damage increases by 1.

riders.label: Metal armor: persistent electricity
riders.note: Only if the target wears metal armor or is made of metal (you also get +1 on the attack roll)

name: Siphoning Rivulet
trigger: A creature within range takes persistent bleed damage.
description: You seize hold of the target's blood as it leaves its body, siphoning it through the air to yourself. The target increases the triggering bleed damage by 4d4, and you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the total bleed damage they took that lasts until the end of your next turn.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d4.

name: Sleep
description: Each creature in the area becomes drowsy, possibly nodding off. A creature that falls unconscious from this spell doesn't fall prone or release what it's holding. This spell doesn't prevent creatures from waking up due to a successful Perception check, limiting its utility in combat.

degrees.Success: The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

heightened.4th: The creatures fall unconscious for 1 round on a failure or 1 minute on a critical failure. They fall prone and release what they're holding, and they can't attempt Perception checks to wake up. When the duration ends, the creature is sleeping normally instead of automatically waking up.

name: Songshroud
description: You surround yourself in a beautiful melody that echoes in unusual directions, confounding attempts to hear your precise location. While using songshroud, you can’t become unnoticed by creatures that can hear. You are hidden to creatures that rely purely on echolocation or other sound-based senses, and you can’t become observed by those senses unless they use magic or another special ability and succeed at a counteract check against this spell; as such, you can use songshroud in combination with becoming invisible (or foiling vision in a different way) to avoid being observed by a creature with both vision and echolocation. The shroud also protects you from harmful sounds, granting you a +1 status bonus to saving throws against auditory effects and resistance 5 to sonic damage. When you speak, you can choose to either project your voice normally or to use the song to hide it. If you choose to hide it, creatures that are farther than 5 feet away must succeed at a Perception check against your spell DC to overhear what you are saying.

heightened.4th: The resistance to sonic damage increases to 10, the duration increases to 10 minutes, and the status bonus on saving throws against auditory effects increases to +2.
heightened.7th: The resistance to sonic damage increases to 15, the duration increases to 10 minutes, and the status bonus on saving throws against auditory effects increases to +3.

name: Spirit Boundary
description:
You call forth a stationary shield of defensive spirits that blunt dangerous forces that cross its boundaries. The spirits do not impede sight, movement, or line of effect. When an effect that deals damage passes through the spirit shield, reduce the damage that the effect deals by the listed amount; the shield takes the same amount of damage that it reduced. If the crossing effect deals damage to multiple creatures, the shield only takes damage once. When the shield is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it is destroyed. The size of the shield, the amount of damage reduced, and the shield’s durability depend upon the number of actions used to cast the spell.

Single Action The shield has a 10-foot radius and 20 Hit Points. It reduces damage that crosses it by 5.

Two Actions The shield has a 20-foot radius and 50 Hit Points. It reduces damage that crosses it by 10.

Three Actions You can select any radius between 5 feet and 30 feet for the shield. The shield has 50 Hit Points and reduces damage that crosses it by 10. You can Dismiss this spell as a reaction triggered by a damaging effect passing through it.

heightened.+1: For the one-action version, increase the damage reduced by 1 and the shield's Hit Points by 4. For the two-action and three-action versions, increase the damage reduced by 2 and the shield's Hit Points by 12.

name: Spiritual Armament
description: You create a ghostly, magical echo of one weapon you're wielding or wearing and fling it. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target's AC, dealing 2d8 damage on a hit (or double damage on a critical hit).
