The damage type is the same as the chosen weapon (or any of its types for a versatile weapon). The attack deals spirit damage instead if that would be more detrimental to the creature (as determined by the GM). This attack uses and contributes to your multiple attack penalty. After the attack, the weapon returns to your side. If you sanctify the spell, the attacks are sanctified as well. Each time you Sustain the spell, you can repeat the attack against any creature within 120 feet.

name: Steal the Show
description: Your enchanting performance draws everyone’s attention to you, for good or for ill. Opponents have an easier time targeting you, gaining a +1 status bonus to attack rolls against you. However, the distraction makes it more difficult for them to defend themselves and perform more complex tasks. If you Sustain the spell, creatures attempt a new saving throw at the beginning of each of their turns.

degrees.Critical Success: The creature gains the status bonus to attack rolls against you for the spell’s duration and doesn’t need to attempt further saving throws.
degrees.Success: The creature is off-guard against everyone except you for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The creature is stupefied 2 and off-guard against everyone except you for 1 round.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but the creature is instead stupefied 4.

name: Stupefy
description: You dull the target's mind, depending on its Will save.

degrees.Success: The target is stupefied 1 until the start of your next turn.
degrees.Failure: The target is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target is stupefied 3 for 1 minute.

name: Sudden Bolt
description: You call down a small bolt of lightning on the target, dealing 4d12 electricity damage.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d12.

name: Suggestion
description: Your honeyed words are difficult for creatures to resist. You suggest a course of action to the target, which must be phrased in such a way as to seem like a logical course of action to the target and can't be self-destructive or obviously against the target's self-interest. The target must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Critical Success: The target is unaffected and knows you tried to control it.
degrees.Success: The target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The target immediately follows your suggestion. The spell has a duration of 1 minute, or until the target has completed a finite suggestion or the suggestion becomes self-destructive or has other obvious negative effects.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, but the base duration is 1 hour.

heightened.8th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

name: Summon Undead
description: You summon a creature that has the undead trait and whose level is –1 to fight for you.

heightened.2nd: The creature can be up to Level 1.
heightened.3rd: The creature can be up to Level 2.
heightened.4th: The creature can be up to Level 3.
heightened.5th: The creature can be up to Level 5.
heightened.6th: The creature can be up to Level 7.
heightened.7th: The creature can be up to Level 9.
heightened.8th: The creature can be up to Level 11.
heightened.9th: The creature can be up to Level 13.
heightened.10th: The creature can be up to Level 15.

name: Sure Strike
duration: until the end of your turn
description: The next time you make an attack roll before the end of your turn, roll it twice and use the better result. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being concealed or hidden. You are then temporarily immune to sure strike for 10 minutes.

name: Synaptic Pulse
description: You emit a pulsating mental blast that penetrates the minds of all enemies in the area. Each enemy in the area must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Success: The creature is stunned 1.
degrees.Failure: The creature is stunned 2.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is stunned for 1 round.

name: Targeted Ward
description:
You prepare your defenses against a specific foe, shrouding them in a cloak of divinatory magic that allows you to react to their past actions, focus sharply on their present actions, or peer slightly into their future. You gain a status bonus to your choice of AC, Fortitude saves, Reflex saves, or Will saves against that opponent based on the results of the opponent’s Will save. The number of actions you spend determines when the bonus applies.

Single Action The range is 30 feet. You focus on a specific action the opponent has taken in the past, such as a breath weapon or a Strike. The bonus only applies when the opponent uses that same action against you.

Two Actions The range is 30 feet, and the bonus applies every time that opponent targets your chosen defense.

Three Actions As the two-action version, except the range is 60 feet. Additionally, when the opponent uses an offensive ability against you that targets a different defense (such as targeting your AC after you have chosen to gain a status bonus to Fortitude saves), you can change your defense to match the one appropriate for the incoming attack as a free action. Once you have changed the defense type once, you can’t change it again during the same casting of targeted ward.

degrees.Critical Success: You do not gain a bonus.
degrees.Success: The status bonus is +1.
degrees.Failure: The status bonus is +2.
degrees.Critical Failure: The status bonus is +3.

heightened.5th: Increase the status bonuses granted by each result by 1 (including granting a +1 status bonus on a critical success).

name: Targeting Beacon
description: You create a glowing beacon that hovers over a creature’s body. If the creature was invisible, it becomes concealed instead. If the creature was already concealed for any other reason, it is no longer concealed. While the beacon is active, all creatures gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their attack rolls against the creature. The next time an attack roll hits the creature, in addition to the attack’s normal effects, the beacon explodes, dealing 6d6 fire damage to the creature (basic Reflex save). When the beacon explodes, the spell ends.

heightened.+1: The damage from the exploding beacon increases by 2d6.

name: Telekinetic Hand
targets: 1 unattended object of Light Bulk or less
description: You create a floating, magical hand, either invisible or ghostlike, that grasps the target object and levitates it slowly up to 20 feet in any direction. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the object an additional 20 feet. If the object is in the air when the spell ends, the object falls.

heightened.3rd: You can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.
heightened.5th: The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.
heightened.7th: The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less.

name: Telekinetic Projectile
description: You hurl a loose, unattended object that is within range and that has 1 Bulk or less at the target. Make a spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage—as appropriate for the object you hurled—equal to 1d6 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d6.

name: Telepathy
description: You can communicate telepathically with creatures within 30 feet. Once you establish a connection by communicating with a creature, the communication is two-way. You can communicate only with creatures that share a language with you.

heightened.6th: You can communicate telepathically with creatures using shared mental imagery even if you don't share a language; telepathy loses the linguistic trait.

name: Thousand-Year Flood
description:
You conjure forth a near-mythical deluge of water. The deluge has the following effects:

The area is filled with water, which is greater difficult terrain to all creatures without a swim Speed and difficult terrain for creatures with a swim Speed. Creatures in the area that cannot breathe in water must hold their breath or immediately begin drowning.

When you initially Cast the Spell, creatures in the area are tossed about by the rushing water, taking 10d10 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save.

The water deals 20d12 damage to structures and unattended objects in the area or half this amount if the structure or object is magical.

The first time each round you Sustain this spell, you can spend an action to attack a creature in the area with debris and rushing water, making a spell attack roll against their AC and dealing 9d4 bludgeoning damage on a success, or double damage on a critical success.

Special If you are a follower of a deity with the water domain, you can choose to give this spell the sanctified trait.

name: Time Skip
description: You grasp on to a creature’s place in the timestream, pulling them a moment into the future. Temporal friction grinds against their body as the timestream rights itself, and they might be disoriented from the displacement. The target takes 3d6 force damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, they are also slowed 1.

heightened.+1: The force damage increases by 2d6.

name: Tortoise and the Hare
targets: 1 foe, and either yourself or 1 ally
description: You prove that slow and steady doesn't always win the race by turning a foe into a proverbial tortoise and yourself or an ally into a hare, siphoning some of the foe's speed and granting it to the other target. The effects depend on the foe's Fortitude save.

degrees.Success: The foe is slowed 1 for 1 round, and the other target is unaffected.
degrees.Failure: The foe is slowed 1 for 3 rounds and the other target is quickened for 1 round. It can use the extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.
degrees.Critical Failure: The foe is slowed 1 for 3 minutes and the other target is quickened for 1 minute. It can use the extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.

name: Translate
description: The target can understand the meaning of a single language it is hearing or reading when you Cast the Spell. This doesn't let it understand codes, language couched in metaphor, and the like (subject to GM discretion). If the target can hear multiple languages and knows that, it can choose which language to understand; otherwise, choose one of the languages randomly.

heightened.3rd: The target can also speak the language.
heightened.4th: You can target up to 10 creatures, and targets can also speak the language.

name: Truesight
description: You see things within 60 feet as they actually are. The GM rolls a secret counteract check against any illusion, morph, or polymorph effect in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it (for instance, if the check succeeds against a polymorph spell, you can see the creature's true form, but you don't end the polymorph spell).

name: Umbral Escape
description:
Requirements You are in bright or dim light.

You merge with your shadow and displace yourself to another shadow within range. Stride up to your Speed towards another creature or object with a shadow. You ignore difficult terrain during this Stride and can move along solid surfaces like walls or ceilings during this movement. You gain resistance 4 to all damage during this Stride. You are then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

heightened.+1: Increase the resistance by 4.

name: Unhelpful Nudge
trigger: A creature off-guard to you attempts to Stride.
description: With but a thought and a wave of your hand, you cause an unprepared enemy to move. The target creature attempts a Reflex save. You push the creature 10 feet at any point during its Stride. If the creature would be pushed into another creature or object, it must attempt a Reflex save, falling prone if it fails its save.

name: Ventriloquism
description: Whenever you speak or make any other sound vocally, you can make your vocalization seem to originate from somewhere else within 60 feet, and you can change that apparent location freely as you vocalize. Any creature that hears the sound can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

heightened.2nd: The spell's duration increases to 1 hour, and you can also change the tone, quality, and other aspects of your voice. Before a creature can attempt to disbelieve your illusion, it must actively attempt a Perception check or otherwise use actions to interact with the sound.

name: Volley My Ally
description:
Requirements You are wielding a ranged weapon or thrown weapon and could make a Strike with it.

You meld your ally into a ranged weapon you are holding for a combo assault. You imbue your ally into the required weapon and make a ranged Strike at a creature within the required weapon’s first range increment. Your ally then appears in a space adjacent to the target of your Strike (or the closest available space if there are no available adjacent spaces). If your ally could make a melee Strike at the end of this movement against the enemy you targeted with your Strike, it may spend its reaction to make a Strike against the enemy.

heightened.+2: Increase the range of the Strike by 15 feet.

name: Wall of Time
description:
You conjure up a wall of slowed time, which is both transparent and sepia-colored. The wall is 1 inch thick, up to 120 feet long, and up to 20 feet high. You can shape the wall’s path, placing each 5 feet of the wall on the border between squares. The wall doesn’t need to stand vertically or be supported by any surface. You must conjure the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don’t pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost.
Mindless creatures, unattended objects (including thrown weapons and ammunition), and spell effects that pass through the wall are trapped within, emerging 1 round after they enter it. Held objects that enter the wall, such as a melee weapon used to Strike a creature on the other side of the wall, become stuck for 1 round as well. The creature can choose to drop the held object; if they do, the object continues its trajectory after it is freed from the wall’s effect; this would allow a melee weapon to resolve the Strike before falling to the ground. However, attacks and targeted effects whose targets have moved from their original position since they entered the wall automatically miss.
Non-mindless creatures that enter the wall of time lose one action from their current turn if they can; if they can’t, either because they are on their final action for the turn or it is not their turn, they are instead slowed 1.

name: Wave of Despair
description: You inflict despair on creatures in the area. The effects for each creature are determined by its Will save.

degrees.Success: For 1 round, the creature can't use reactions and must attempt another save at the start of its turn; on a failure, it is slowed 1 for that turn as it sobs uncontrollably.
degrees.Failure: As success, but the duration is 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, and the creature is automatically slowed 1 for 1 minute.

heightened.7th: The area increases to a 60-foot cone.

name: Winter's Cold Embrace
description: You create a darkness so cold and chilling that even those that would call the cold and darkness friend fear it. Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a torch or lantern, do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical light of the spell's rank or lower. Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. From outside, it appears as a frosted globe of pure darkness. Creatures with darkvision cannot see through the darkness and creatures with greater darkness still treat the area as concealed. Creatures that end their turn in the area of darkness take 12d4 cold damage with a basic Fortitude save. Creatures with immunities or resistance to cold damage take spirit damage instead if they do not possess darkvision or greater darkvision.

heightened.+1: Increase the damage by 2d4.

name: Winter's Grasp
description: You infuse the ground with the essence of winter, coating it in supernaturally cold ice. All creatures in the area of the spell when you first cast it take 4d6 cold damage (basic Reflex save). You can Sustain this spell. While the spell lasts, the icy ground is difficult terrain, and each creature standing on the ice must succeed at their choice of a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check against your spell DC or fall prone. Creatures using an action to move onto the ice during the spell’s duration must also attempt their choice of either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to Balance. A creature that ends its turn standing on the ice takes 8 cold damage with a basic Fortitude save.

heightened.+1: Increase the cold damage that creatures take from the initial casting by 1d6 and the cold damage they take from ending their turn in the ice by 2.
